message:
docs: add API doc comments across 36 public headers and reconcile stale counts

Add concise Doxygen-style doc comments to undocumented public API
functions across 36 header files spanning all major subsystem families:
core (broadcast, circuit_breaker, epoch, oneshot, session, watch),
runtime (blocking, builder, deadline_monitor, io_driver, lab, rt, waker),
sync (barrier, notify, once, semaphore), net (db, http, net, pipe, quic),
actor/supervisor, bytes/codec, cli, codec, console, evidence,
evidence_sink, fs, link, monitor, observability, process, signal,
stream, and tracing_compat.

Each comment follows the existing one-line "/* Verb ... */" convention
and describes what the function does, not how.

Also reconcile stale hard-coded counts in README.md and CHANGELOG.md:
- Replace "28 subsystems" with prose ("broad subsystem surface") to
  avoid future drift as families are added.
- Update header count from 95 to 122 and source count from 95 to 123
  to reflect the current tree.

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with two closed doc issues (bd-3ueu, bd-qxtx).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3vt","title":"EPIC: Advanced Alpha Levers and Research Backlog (No Feature Loss)","description":"Background:
The plan includes advanced ideas (formal assurance ladder, lock-free alternatives, cache-oblivious layouts, etc.). These should be pursued via controlled spikes with rollback and proof artifacts.

Goal:
Run high-EV research tasks that can elevate performance/assurance without semantic compromise.

Constraints:
- one lever at a time
- explicit baseline/profile/isomorphism artifacts
- fallback path mandatory

Why this serves users:
- keeps project ambitious without destabilizing the core delivery path.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.632150816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850235463Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850215876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","optimization","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.1","title":"Pilot formal assurance ladder from golden outputs to bounded model checks to translation validation","description":"Background:
The plan calls for replacing missing compile-time guarantees with layered assurance artifacts.

Subtasks:
- Define staged assurance levels and entry/exit criteria.
- Pilot bounded model checks on selected lifecycle/state-machine components.
- Define lightweight translation-validation checks for critical transformations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Produces concrete recommendation with cost/benefit and integration path.
- Includes fallback plan if tooling cost outweighs value.
- Leaves behind reusable templates for future formal efforts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.410198918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:50:56.417210097Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:50:56.417190430Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["assurance","formal","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.10","title":"Prototype optional Rust-AST zero-drift extractor for transition and invariant artifacts","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.3B allows an optional zero-drift extractor path to reduce manual transcription drift from Rust semantics.

Subtasks:
- Build minimal tooling prototype (Rust AST parser under `tools/`) that emits transition X-macros and invariant schema fragments.
- Compare generated artifacts against manually curated baseline for one subsystem.
- Define promotion criteria, review requirements, and rollback plan if extractor proves brittle.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates measurable drift-reduction value on sampled subsystem.
- Generated artifacts pass parity review before any adoption.
- Clear go/no-go decision and risk note are recorded.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:49:24.000169440Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:49:24.000147309Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","research","spec","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-hwb.5","title":"Implement ghost safety monitors for protocol and linearity invariants","description":"Background:
Ghost monitors provide lightweight runtime checks to recover part of Rust-level protocol confidence in debug/hardened modes.

Subtasks:
- Implement protocol monitor for lifecycle sequencing violations.
- Implement linearity monitor for reserve/commit/abort misuse.
- Integrate monitor outputs into test diagnostics and traces.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Monitor violations are deterministic and actionable.
- Overhead behavior is documented per safety profile.
- Tests demonstrate detection of known violation patterns.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:47:21.005276348Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:47:21.005258073Z","close_reason":"Ghost safety monitors fully implemented and tested: protocol monitor for lifecycle sequencing violations (region/task/obligation transitions), linearity monitor for obligation double-use and leak detection, ring buffer with overflow handling, query interface, and integration with lifecycle/scheduler/quiescence. 21 tests, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ghost","invariants","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.6","title":"Implement strict resource-contract engine and deterministic exhaustion behavior","description":"Background:
Resource exhaustion is a major C failure mode. The plan requires deterministic, non-corrupting behavior at all boundaries.

Subtasks:
- Implement configurable ceilings (memory, queue depth, timer nodes, trace events).
- Implement admission/backpressure/reject decision points with stable error codes.
- Add failure-atomic rollback semantics at boundary transitions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No partial state corruption on exhaustion paths.
- Boundary tests cover arena alloc, queue growth, timer ops, and obligation ops.
- Behavior is consistent across profiles and reproducible in replay.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.229496469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:03:07.143819484Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:03:07.143801410Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","oom","phase-3","resource"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-296.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-296.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.7","title":"Finalize error stability policy, safety profiles, and deterministic time/entropy fault modes","description":"Background:
Error policy and deterministic boundary rules are core substitutes for Rust-level guarantees in C.

Subtasks:
- Define canonical error families and compatibility policy (stable meanings, additive growth rules).
- Implement compiler-enforced must-use behavior for transition/acquisition APIs.
- Implement zero-allocation contextual error ledger (`file/line/op`) for deterministic failure propagation breadcrumbs.
- Define safety profiles (debug-ghost/hardened/release-fast) with explicit invariants and overhead envelopes.
- Implement deterministic time/entropy/clock-anomaly fault injection hooks and fixtures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Error policy is versioned and documented.
- Must-use and context-ledger behavior are test-covered and deterministic.
- Each safety profile has explicit invariants and intended use.
- Fault-injection paths are covered by automated tests linked to replay artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.314994587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:12:39.197458880Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:12:39.197440485Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","errors","phase-3","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-hwb.9","title":"Implement security and safety posture checks for pointer ownership and invariant violation handling","description":"Background:
Section 12 now includes explicit threat-model boundaries and production fault-containment expectations in addition to debug fail-fast behavior.

Subtasks:
- Document and enforce pointer ownership and stale-handle misuse defenses.
- Add misuse tests for illegal transitions, stale handles, unsafe pointer paths, and malformed-input boundaries.
- Codify in-scope vs out-of-scope threat boundaries for kernel responsibilities.
- Implement deterministic production containment path (region poison/isolation policy) so failures can be scoped without undefined behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Safety posture requirements are test-enforced and profile-aware.
- Failure behavior is deterministic, visible, and bounded by policy.
- Security-relevant misuse paths have regression coverage with reproducible artifacts.
- Threat boundary documentation maps directly to tests and explicit non-goals.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:10.119904097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:57:17.513999481Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:57:17.513978090Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","safety","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8","title":"EPIC: Build System, Portability Matrix, and Repository Scaffold","description":"Background:
The runtime must compile and behave predictably across host and constrained targets. Build portability is a first-class requirement, not cleanup work.

Goal:
Establish build/repo infrastructure for GCC/Clang/MSVC plus embedded cross-target matrix, including QEMU validation harness.

Must preserve:
- dependency-free core assumptions
- profile-aware compile paths
- reproducible, diagnosable failures

Why this serves users:
- HFT teams need deterministic reproducible builds
- embedded teams need cross-target confidence before deployment
- maintainers need fast feedback loops with clear failure surfaces.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:41.970107711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:52:13.326969139Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:52:13.326946788Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","phase-2","portability"]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.1","title":"Scaffold repository layout and module boundaries for include/src/tests/fixtures/tools/docs","description":"Background:
The codebase structure must enforce architectural boundaries from day one to prevent accidental coupling.

Subtasks:
- Create canonical directory map for include, src, tests, fixtures, tools, docs.
- Define ownership for each module boundary and allowed dependencies.
- Define naming conventions for APIs, internal files, and test fixtures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Layout aligns with proposed architecture and parity tracking needs.
- No ambiguous module ownership.
- New contributors can infer where code belongs without guessing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.042079992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:35:54.621282796Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:35:54.621264252Z","close_reason":"Completed scaffold contract details: canonical directory map, explicit module ownership/dependency boundaries, and naming conventions in docs/PHASE2_SCAFFOLD_MANIFEST.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","phase-2","structure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Added to Makefile and asx_runtime_reset. 18 unit tests all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reg6","title":"EPIC: Rust Reference Fixture Capture Pipeline","description":"## Goal
Populate fixtures/rust_reference/ with golden outputs captured from the pinned Rust baseline
(commit 38c152405bd03e2bd9eecf178bfbbe9472fed861, toolchain nightly-2026-02-19).

## Why This Matters
The conformance gate currently runs against the C implementation's own fixtures, which validates
internal consistency but NOT Rust-vs-C behavioral parity. Without Rust-captured reference fixtures,
we cannot prove the C port produces identical semantic outputs for identical inputs. This is the
single highest-value remaining gap — it converts "self-consistent" into "provably equivalent."

## What "Done" Looks Like
- fixtures/rust_reference/ contains captured golden outputs for all 14 E2E fixture families (85+ scenarios)
- Each fixture has full provenance metadata (rust_baseline_commit, toolchain_commit_hash, cargo_lock_sha256)
- make conformance --fixtures-root fixtures/rust_reference passes with 0 semantic deltas
- Fixture schema validation passes against schemas/canonical_fixture.schema.json

## Architectural Context
The conformance infrastructure (tools/ci/run_conformance.sh) already supports --fixtures-root pointing
at Rust-captured data. The fixture schema, provenance map (docs/SOURCE_TO_FIXTURE_PROVENANCE_MAP.md,
28 provenance rows), and semantic delta budget policy (docs/SEMANTIC_DELTA_BUDGET.md, budget=0) are
all in place. This epic is purely about running the Rust side and capturing output.

## Risk
- The pinned Rust baseline may not build cleanly on current nightly (last verified 2026-02-26)
- Cargo.lock SHA must match exactly: 77f4bfaedfe5b8572dfca3181119f9432825ea3d868ee8f5d2793a6c5cdeffdc
- Any Rust-vs-C semantic delta discovered here would be a real bug (in either direction)","notes":"## IMPORTANT: FIXTURE DIRECTORY IS NOT EMPTY (2026-03-12 audit)

The fixtures/rust_reference/ directory IS populated with 7 families:
- smoke/ (10 fixtures), core_budget/, core_cancel/, core_channel/,
  core_lifecycle/, core_timer/, deployment_hardening/

HOWEVER, these are PLACEHOLDER/SCAFFOLD fixtures generated by the C implementation:
- semantic_digest values are "sha256:aaaaaaa..." (all-a placeholder)
- expected_events are empty arrays
- Provenance fields reference the correct Rust baseline but fixtures were NOT
  generated by actually running the Rust binary

The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION report (Gap #1) is correct: no actual Rust-generated
fixtures exist. The directory contains structural scaffolds.

## What This Epic Does
Replace placeholder fixtures with REAL Rust-generated golden outputs by:
1. Setting up Rust build environment
2. Building the Rust binary in fixture capture mode
3. Running it to generate real fixtures (replacing placeholders)
4. Validating the real fixtures against the schema
5. Running C conformance against real Rust fixtures (first true parity test)
6. E2E test verifying the entire pipeline

## Dependency Structure
Linear: .1 → .2 → .3 → .4 → .5, then .6 (e2e test) depends on .5

## Cross-Epic Dependencies
- bd-rkql depends on bd-reg6 (fuzzing needs Rust binary)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":600,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:05:50.816361571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:40:12.333091287Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:40:12.331096181Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","phase3"]}
